description_long: This is a tall, multi-tiered pagoda structure with five distinct stories, each marked by wide, gently sloping roofs that curve upward at the eaves. The roofs are dark gray, with subtle ridges and red ornamental details at the corners, giving the structure a striking, traditional appearance. At the apex, a prominent black spire with a series of stacked discs serves as an ornamental finial, a hallmark of East Asian pagoda architecture. The body of the pagoda is mostly obscured by the overhanging roofs, but glimpses of red structural elements are visible beneath each tier. The overall silhouette is symmetrical and elegant, with each level slightly smaller than the one below, creating a tapered effect. This type of structure is commonly found in temple complexes, parks, or as a cultural landmark in urban settings.
description: A five-story traditional pagoda with upward-curving dark gray roofs, red accents, and a tall ornamental spire at the top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical five-tiered roofs, prominent upward-curving eaves, and the central spire.
description_view_1: Side view with similar features, but the roof layers and red corner details are more visible along the edges.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, highlighting the stacked roofs and central spire.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with clear visibility of the roof tiers and red accents.
